description_long: This is a medium-sized rectangular cardboard parcel, commonly used for shipping and delivery in urban environments. The box is constructed from brown corrugated cardboard, featuring a subtle vertical linear texture across its surfaces. A single, wide strip of black packing tape runs along the top seam, securing the flaps and providing a visual contrast to the otherwise uniform brown exterior. The box appears to be fully sealed, with no visible openings, labels, or markings aside from the tape. Its proportions suggest it could contain a variety of goods, making it suitable for use by courier services, postal deliveries, or moving companies. The overall appearance is utilitarian and typical of standard shipping parcels found on doorsteps, in mailrooms, or at logistics centers.
description: A brown cardboard shipping box with a single black tape strip sealing the top. The surface is textured and unmarked, giving it a standard, utilitarian look.
description_view_0: The front view shows the brown cardboard face with the black tape strip running vertically along the top edge.
description_view_1: This side view displays the box's broad face and the black tape running horizontally across the top seam.
description_view_2: The rear view mirrors the front, with the black tape strip again visible along the top edge.
description_view_3: This side view is similar to view 1, showing the broad face and the black tape running horizontally across the top.
